text: Welcome everyone. Today we begin Chapter 7 on linear transformations.
speaker: Host

text: A linear map T from vector space V to W preserves vector addition and scalar multiplication.
speaker: Host

text: Remember the kernel, or null space, is the set of vectors that map to zero.
speaker: Host

text: The rank-nullity theorem says dimension of V equals rank of T plus nullity of T.
speaker: Host

text: This is critical for the midterm: you must be able to prove this in both finite dimensions.
speaker: Host

text: Let's work an example with a 3 by 3 matrix representing T in the standard basis.
speaker: Host

text: Row reduce to find pivots; the number of pivot columns is the rank.
speaker: Host

text: Any questions before we move on to eigenvalues?
speaker: Host

text: Eigenvectors are non-zero vectors v such that T v equals lambda v for some scalar lambda.
speaker: Host

text: The characteristic polynomial is det of A minus lambda I equals zero.
speaker: Host

text: We will connect this to diagonalization next lecture.
speaker: Host
